rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Banned words — grep zero-tolerance
Ritual / practice (as noun) / ceremony — hard ban
No stale markers, no 'fixed' retrospectives, no session biography
Register match
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 5: 'Run the policy files raw against the system you built'; line 49: 'the check carries judgment about what matters in this system'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No combative verbs about the agent in body prose
Training creator's name banned in student-facing body
Author-'we' ban — first-person-plural extension
Over-hedge detector
Value-prop / positioning framing belongs in sales copy + strategy docs, NOT in student-facing curriculum body
Watch out for planting jargon too early
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 9: 'skill packages an expertise (rules, checklists, the moves an agent runs) into something the agent can load and use' — term earned at point of use with concrete definition

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Earn every technical term on first use
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 9: 'A skill packages an expertise...'; line 52: 'The reusable check carries two lenses inside one personal skill'; lines 65-72 name risk patterns explicitly before use

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Business-audience word ban (Bootstrap default)
Inline links use the target's title, never its filename
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 144: '[Audit your agent](audit-your-agent.md)' uses exercise title

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No placeholders inside fenced blocks
rules_evaluated.evidence: All four prompt blocks contain no [...] placeholders; Phase 1 line 24 'Be specific. Be plain.' is guidance, not a placeholder

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 17: 'Reference material lives in...'; line 37: 'Now add judgment.'; line 57: 'Ask Claude to author both lenses...'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Canonical presentation shape
rules_evaluated.evidence: All prompts follow pattern: lead-in sentence, **Prompt** *(Claude Code)*, blank line, fenced block

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Installed Claude Code Skills invoke by name, never by path
rules_evaluated.evidence: Prompts do not invoke skills by path; 'module-4/policies/' is a data-source path, not a skill invocation

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Artifact destinations are explicit subpaths, never naked filenames (Bootstrap-specific)
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 24: 'outputs/policy-report-raw.md'; line 40: 'outputs/policy-report-raw.md'; line 62: 'module-4/skills/security-audit/' — all explicit subpaths

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 110-112: 'The point' section names the lesson (expert in the room, packaging, forcing function) — mood contract is implicit in the teaching structure (moving from raw policy files to reusable check is the arc)

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity check

todo_items.text: Phase 3 prompt includes CLI/Desktop standalone-install wording. Decide whether to label it as a prompt or teach the extractor to capture 'Use this shape...' blocks.
Mechanical re-run owed before first cohort delivery for runner pair bootstrap-m4-author.

summary: File passes all writing-class compendiums. No banned words; register stays peer-flat with Rory-reframe undertone (Godin×Sutherland×Siilasmaa trio). All technical terms earned at point of use (skill, policy lens, agent-security lens, named risk patterns). All prompt blocks follow canonical shape with action lead-ins; artifact destinations are explicit Bootstrap subpaths (outputs/, module-4/); no placeholders. All content above maintainer_cut; no jargon planting; no creator-name leak; no author-we. Two open TODOs in maintainer block are non-blocking (both process-level, not content). Bootstrap voice tone lands consistently across the 40-minute exercise arc.
